title: alaternus balearica
intro: Alaternus balearica, also known as the Bitter Oak or Bitter Holly, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in the management of certain skin conditions. Alaternus balearica is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, although its uses may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
